memes.label: "Agafea Mihalovna"
memes.description: "Konstantin Levin's old nurse and housekeeper at Pokrovskoe, a figure of maternal wisdom and rural constancy who serves as Levin's confidante and the domestic heart of his country estate throughout Anna Karenina."

memes.label: "Alexei Alexandrovitch Karenin"
memes.description: "Alexei Alexandrovitch Karenin is Anna Karenina's husband, a high-ranking government official whose rigid propriety and emotional coldness drive the novel's central marital tragedy."

memes.label: "Alexei Vronsky"
memes.description: "A handsome and wealthy cavalry officer whose passionate affair with Anna Karenina drives the novel's central tragedy."

memes.label: "Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "The tragic heroine of Tolstoy's novel, a married aristocrat whose affair with Count Vronsky leads to her social downfall and suicide."

memes.label: Annushka
memes.description: Anna Karenina's loyal maid and companion, present at key moments of Anna's inner life and final journey.

memes.label: "Captain Yashvin"
memes.description: "A tall, well-built cavalry officer of Vronsky's regiment, a notorious gambler and rake whose blunt honesty and unwavering loyalty make him one of the few steadfast male friendships in Vronsky's life throughout Anna Karenina."

memes.label: Count Bezzubov
memes.description: A French spiritualist and mesmerist adopted by Countess Lydia Ivanovna's circle in St. Petersburg, whose real name is Bezzubov; he assumes the identity of "Landau" as a clairvoyant and influences Alexei Karenin's decisions.

memes.label: "Countess Bola"
memes.description: "A Moscow society hostess in Anna Karenina, known for her musical evenings, whom Levin visits during his re-entry into Moscow social life."

memes.label: "Countess Nordston"
memes.description: "A friend of Kitty Shcherbatskaya, a sharp-witted society lady of St. Petersburg who enjoys chaffing Konstantin Levin and promotes spiritualism, appearing at key social events throughout the novel."

memes.label: Countess Vronskaya
memes.description: Mother of Alexei Vronsky, a fashionable Petersburg society woman whose journey with Anna Karenina to Moscow sets events in motion.

memes.label: "Darya Alexandrovna Oblonsky (Dolly)"
memes.description: "Darya Alexandrovna (Dolly) Oblonsky is Stepan Arkadyevitch's long-suffering wife, the mother of six children, and a moral center of the novel — her domestic endurance, selfless maternal devotion, and quiet dignity represent Tolstoy's ideal of feminine virtue against which Anna's passionate rebellion is implicitly measured."

memes.label: "Family in Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "Family is the central thematic concern of Anna Karenina, established in the novel's famous opening line — happy and unhappy families serve as the prism through which Tolstoy examines Russian society, morality, and human happiness."

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: The nature and function of friendship in Anna Karenina, examining key bonds such as Levin-Stiva, Anna-Dolly, and how friendship operates across gender and class lines.

memes.label: "Front Matter"

memes.label: Golenishtchev
memes.description: Golenishtchev is a comrade of Vronsky from the Corps of Pages, a writer and art critic living in Italy whose intellectual pretensions contrast with Vronsky's practical nature and Mihailov's genuine artistic depth.

memes.label: "The Government Ministry"
memes.description: "The imperial bureaucracy where Alexei Karenin holds a senior position — representing the cold, procedural world that Anna's passion rebels against."

memes.label: Grisha Oblonsky
memes.description: The youngest son of Stepan Arkadyevitch and Darya Alexandrovna Oblonsky, a spirited boy whose childhood moments illuminate family life in the novel.

memes.label: "Hypocrisy"
memes.description: "Hypocrisy in Anna Karenina is the organizing principle of high society — the code that permits discreet transgression while punishing those who refuse to dissemble, making it one of the novel's most sustained objects of moral critique."

memes.label: "Kapitonitch"
memes.description: "Kapitonitch is the tall, elderly hall-porter of the Karenin household, a gentle and loyal servant who shares a warm rapport with young Seryozha and whose silent recognition of Anna during her secret return visit precipitates one of the novel's most emotionally charged domestic scenes."

memes.label: Katavasov
memes.description: Katavasov is a university professor and friend of Konstantin Levin and Sergius Ivanitch, whose scientific rationalism and genial good humor represent the intellectual life of Moscow in contrast to Levin's agricultural preoccupations.

memes.label: "Kitty Shcherbatskaya"
memes.description: "The younger daughter of Prince and Princess Shcherbatsky, who becomes Konstantin Levin's wife after initially refusing his proposal. A central character whose arc traces the journey from romantic idealism to mature love and motherhood."

memes.label: "Konstantin Levin"
memes.description: "A country landowner and co-protagonist of Anna Karenina, whose search for meaning in work, love, and faith parallels Anna's tragic arc."

memes.label: "Korney"
memes.description: "Korney Vassilievitch is Alexei Karenin's valet, a former soldier who becomes the de facto manager of the Karenin household after Anna's departure, quietly steering domestic affairs and serving as a discreet intermediary between his master and the world outside."

memes.label: Kouzma
memes.description: Konstantin Levin's loyal old servant and valet, a steady and unflappable presence who has served Levin since before his marriage and witnesses the master's emotional life from close quarters.

memes.label: Jules Landau (Count Bezzubov)
memes.description: Jules Landau, also known as Count Bezzubov, is a French medium and spiritual advisor in Countess Lydia Ivanovna's circle, whose mystical pretensions are treated with skeptical humor by Stepan Arkadyevitch and represent the novel's critique of fashionable Petersburg spirituality.

memes.label: Levin–Anna Parallel Structure
memes.description: An analysis of Tolstoy's double-plot architecture in Anna Karenina — how Levin's spiritual ascent and Anna's tragic descent form two halves of a single argument about love, meaning, and the conditions of a worthwhile life.

memes.label: "Levin and Kitty's Wedding"
memes.description: "The wedding of Konstantin Levin and Kitty Shcherbatskaya is one of Anna Karenina's most joyful set pieces — a detailed portrait of the Russian Orthodox marriage rite that anchors the novel's vision of love sanctified by tradition and community."

memes.label: "Levin's Estate"
memes.description: "Konstantin Levin's country estate — the novel's pastoral counterpoint to urban society, where authentic work, family life, and spiritual seeking become possible."

memes.label: Liza Merkalova
memes.description: Liza Merkalova is a beautiful young society woman in Betsy Tverskaya's Petersburg circle whose frank charm and ambiguous marital arrangement exemplify the moral fluidity of high society in Anna Karenina.

memes.label: "Lizaveta Petrovna"
memes.description: "The midwife who attends Kitty's childbirth in Anna Karenina, a calm and competent professional whose steady presence guides the Levin household through a moment of crisis."

memes.label: "Love and Passion in Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "Tolstoy's examination of romantic love, from socialized desire to destructive passion to the grounded love of shared life."

memes.label: Arseny Lvov

memes.label: "Countess Lydia Ivanovna"
memes.description: "Countess Lydia Ivanovna is a Petersburg society woman and religious mystic who becomes Alexei Karenin's spiritual advisor and confidante after Anna leaves him, wielding significant influence over both Karenin's personal life and his official affairs."

memes.label: Madame Kartasova
memes.description: Madame Kartasova is a thin little society woman who publicly humiliates Anna Karenina at the theater by making a scene in the adjoining box, catalyzing Anna's social downfall in Petersburg.

memes.label: "Mademoiselle Linon"
memes.description: "The French governess in the Shcherbatsky household, a minor but recurring figure in Kitty's domestic world during the early parts of Anna Karenina."

memes.label: "Marriage in Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "Tolstoy's exploration of marriage as an institution, contrasting the Oblonskys' hollow union, the Karenins' cold arrangement, and Levin and Kitty's authentic partnership."

memes.label: Marya Nikolaevna
memes.description: Nikolai Levin's common-law partner, a former prostitute who cares for him through his final illness with tenderness and devotion.

memes.label: "Marya Philimonovna"
memes.description: "Marya (Matrona) Philimonovna is the Oblonsky family's old nurse and housekeeper, described by the narrator as 'one inconspicuous but most valuable and useful person' — the practical genius whose competence and good sense hold the household together through Dolly's domestic crises."

memes.label: Masha
memes.description: Servant in Nikolai Levin's household who assists in his care during his final illness, bringing supper and helping Konstantin Levin persuade Nikolai to stay indoors.

memes.label: Matrona Philimonovna
memes.description: The elderly nurse of the Oblonsky household in Moscow, a stern-faced domestic fixture who has served the family through multiple generations of child-rearing.

memes.label: Matvey
memes.description: Matvey is Stepan Arkadyevitch Oblonsky's valet, an old and trusted servant whose quiet competence and knowing glances provide an intimate portrait of the master-servant relationship and a touch of comic warmth in the novel's opening chapters.

memes.label: Metrov
memes.description: Metrov is a celebrated scholar and author of an article on labor whom Levin meets through Katavasov; his intellectual arrogance and inability to engage with Levin's ideas exemplify the limitations of academic discourse in the novel.

memes.label: "Mihailov"
memes.description: "A struggling Russian painter living in Italy, whose encounter with Vronsky, Anna, and Golenishtchev in Part Five of Anna Karenina becomes the vehicle for Tolstoy's meditation on true art versus fashionable dilettantism."

memes.label: "Moscow"
memes.description: "The ancient Russian capital where much of Anna Karenina's early action unfolds — home to the Oblonskys, the Shtcherbatskys, and Levin's social world."

memes.label: "Motherhood"
memes.description: "Motherhood in Anna Karenina is explored through the contrasting experiences of Dolly Oblonsky's self-sacrificing maternal devotion, Anna's divided maternal identity, and Kitty's idealized entry into motherhood — together forming Tolstoy's most sustained meditation on what it means to be a mother."

memes.label: "Nikolai Levin"
memes.description: "The elder brother of Konstantin Levin, a radical intellectual whose dissolute life and fatal illness serve as a foil to Levin's own spiritual and moral journey in Tolstoy's Anna Karenina."

memes.description: "As he rode up to the house in the happiest frame of mind, Levin heard the bell ring at the side of the principal entrance of the house."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 15"
memes.description: "The place fixed on for the stand-shooting was not far above a stream in a little aspen copse. On reaching the copse, Levin got out of the trap and led Oblonsky to a corner of a mossy, swampy glade, al…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 16"
memes.description: "On the way home Levin asked all details of Kitty’s illness and the Shtcherbatskys’ plans, and though he would have been ashamed to admit it, he was pleased at what he heard."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 17"
memes.description: "Stepan Arkadyevitch went upstairs with his pocket bulging with notes, which the merchant had paid him for three months in advance."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 18"
memes.description: "Although all Vronsky’s inner life was absorbed in his passion, his external life unalterably and inevitably followed along the old accustomed lines of his social and regimental ties and interests."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 19"
memes.description: "On the day of the races at Krasnoe Selo, Vronsky had come earlier than usual to eat beefsteak in the common messroom of the regiment."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 2"
memes.description: "Soon after the doctor, Dolly had arrived. She knew that there was to be a consultation that day, and though she was only just up after her confinement (she had another baby, a little girl, born at the…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 20"
memes.description: "Vronsky was staying in a roomy, clean, Finnish hut, divided into two by a partition. Petritsky lived with him in camp too. Petritsky was asleep when Vronsky and Yashvin came into the hut."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 21"
memes.description: "The temporary stable, a wooden shed, had been put up close to the race course, and there his mare was to have been taken the previous day. He had not yet seen her there."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 22"
memes.description: "The rain did not last long, and by the time Vronsky arrived, his shaft-horse trotting at full speed and dragging the trace-horses galloping through the mud, with their reins hanging loose, the sun had…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 23"
memes.description: "Vronsky had several times already, though not so resolutely as now, tried to bring her to consider their position, and every time he had been confronted by the same superficiality and triviality with …"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 24"
memes.description: "When Vronsky looked at his watch on the Karenins’ balcony, he was so greatly agitated and lost in his thoughts that he saw the figures on the watch’s face, but could not take in what time it was."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 25"
memes.description: "There were seventeen officers in all riding in this race. The race course was a large three-mile ring of the form of an ellipse in front of the pavilion."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 26"
memes.description: "The external relations of Alexey Alexandrovitch and his wife had remained unchanged. The sole difference lay in the fact that he was more busily occupied than ever."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 27"
memes.description: "Anna was upstairs, standing before the looking-glass, and, with Annushka’s assistance, pinning the last ribbon on her gown when she heard carriage wheels crunching the gravel at the entrance."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 28"
memes.description: "When Alexey Alexandrovitch reached the race-course, Anna was already sitting in the pavilion beside Betsy, in that pavilion where all the highest society had gathered."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 29"
memes.description: "Everyone was loudly expressing disapprobation, everyone was repeating a phrase someone had uttered—“The lions and gladiators will be the next thing,” and everyone was feeling horrified; so that when V…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 3"
memes.description: "When she went into Kitty’s little room, a pretty, pink little room, full of knick-knacks in *vieux saxe,* as fresh, and pink, and white, and gay as Kitty herself had been two months ago, Dolly remembe…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 30"
memes.description: "In the little German watering-place to which the Shtcherbatskys had betaken themselves, as in all places indeed where people are gathered together, the usual process, as it were, of the crystallizatio…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 31"
memes.description: "It was a wet day; it had been raining all the morning, and the invalids, with their parasols, had flocked into the arcades."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 32"
memes.description: "The particulars which the princess had learned in regard to Varenka’s past and her relations with Madame Stahl were as follows:"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 33"
memes.description: "Kitty made the acquaintance of Madame Stahl too, and this acquaintance, together with her friendship with Varenka, did not merely exercise a great influence on her, it also comforted her in her mental…"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 34"
memes.description: "Before the end of the course of drinking the waters, Prince Shtcherbatsky, who had gone on from Carlsbad to Baden and Kissingen to Russian friends—to get a breath of Russian air, as he said—came back …"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 35"
memes.description: "The prince communicated his good humor to his own family and his friends, and even to the German landlord in whose rooms the Shtcherbatskys were staying."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 4"
memes.description: "The highest Petersburg society is essentially one: in it everyone knows everyone else, everyone even visits everyone else. But this great set has its subdivisions."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 5"
memes.description: "“This is rather indiscreet, but it’s so good it’s an awful temptation to tell the story,” said Vronsky, looking at her with his laughing eyes. “I’m not going to mention any names.”"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 6"
memes.description: "Princess Betsy drove home from the theater, without waiting for the end of the last act. She had only just time to go into her dressing-room, sprinkle her long, pale face with powder, rub it, set her …"

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 7"
memes.description: "Steps were heard at the door, and Princess Betsy, knowing it was Madame Karenina, glanced at Vronsky. He was looking towards the door, and his face wore a strange new expression."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 8"
memes.description: "Alexey Alexandrovitch had seen nothing striking or improper in the fact that his wife was sitting with Vronsky at a table apart, in eager conversation with him about something."

memes.label: "Part Two, Chapter 9"
memes.description: "Anna came in with hanging head, playing with the tassels of her hood. Her face was brilliant and glowing; but this glow was not one of brightness; it suggested the fearful glow of a conflagration in t…"

memes.label: Pestsov
memes.description: Pestsov is a liberal intellectual, a "great talker" and "celebrated enthusiast" whose passionate dinner-table debates on women's rights, civilization, and education illuminate the intellectual ferment of Tolstoy's Russia while also revealing the limits of abstract argument.

memes.label: "Petersburg Society"
memes.description: "Petersburg society in Anna Karenina represents the cold, performative, and morally hollow world of imperial high society — a realm of rigid social codes, bureaucratic ambition, and extra-marital discretion that stands in contrast to Moscow's warmer domesticity and Levin's rural authenticity."

memes.label: "Petritsky"
memes.description: "A young lieutenant of Vronsky's cavalry regiment, Vronsky's favorite comrade and housemate at camp — a good-natured reveler involved in a scandal with a baroness, whose antics provide comic relief in the officers' quarters."

memes.label: "Pokrovskoe"
memes.description: "Pokrovskoe is Konstantin Levin's country estate, the setting for his agricultural experiments, his marriage to Kitty, and his spiritual search — representing Tolstoy's ideal of meaningful rural life."

memes.label: Pride and Humiliation
memes.description: The dynamics of pride and social humiliation in Anna Karenina, examining how pride motivates characters and how public humiliation functions as a social sanction, particularly for female transgression.

memes.label: "Prince Serpuhovskoy"
memes.description: "Vronsky's comrade from the Corps of Pages who has risen to the rank of general, embodying the path of military ambition that Vronsky chose not to pursue — their reunion in Part Three crystallizes Vronsky's conflicted relationship with his own career and social position."

memes.label: "Prince Alexander Shcherbatsky"
memes.description: "The patriarch of the Shcherbatsky family, father of Dolly, Kitty, and Natalia. An old-fashioned Russian aristocrat with a sharp wit and protective nature, who initially favors Levin over Vronsky as a suitor for Kitty."

memes.label: "Prince Tchetchensky"
memes.description: "A well-known figure in the Petersburg club world, Prince Tchetchensky is a 'shlupik' — a permanent fixture of the billiard room — who maintains two families and serves for Stepan Arkadyevitch as an emblem of the libertine vitality of Petersburg life."

memes.label: "Princess Betsy Tverskaya"
memes.description: "A prominent figure in St. Petersburg high society and Anna Karenina's cousin's wife, who facilitates Anna's social life and her fateful encounters with Count Vronsky. A worldly, amoral society hostess whose salon becomes the setting for the novel's central adultery."

memes.label: Princess Marya Borissovna
memes.description: An elderly princess of St. Petersburg high society, Kitty Shcherbatskaya's godmother, at whose house Kitty encounters Vronsky after his return from abroad.

memes.label: Princess Myakaya
memes.description: An elderly society lady noted for her blunt, unconventional wit and her habit of saying exactly what others dare not, making her both a feared and valued presence in Petersburg drawing rooms.

memes.label: "Princess Shcherbatskaya"
memes.description: "The matriarch of the Shcherbatsky family and mother of Dolly, Kitty, and Natalia. A practical, socially ambitious mother whose schemes for her daughters' marriages drive early plot developments before she comes to accept Levin as Kitty's husband."

memes.label: "Princess Sorokina"
memes.description: "A young princess of marriageable age, connected to Countess Vronskaya, who becomes the unwitting object of Anna Karenina's jealous imagination — a figure who exists more powerfully in Anna's mind than she does in the novel's actual social scenes."

memes.label: Princess Varvara
memes.description: Alexei Karenin's aunt who serves as Anna's nominal chaperone during her life with Vronsky, providing a thin veneer of respectability while proving herself a hindrance rather than a comfort to Anna.

memes.label: Pyotr Dmitrievitch
memes.description: The obstetrician summoned to attend Kitty Levin's childbirth, a calm and authoritative medical presence whose reassurance anchors the household during the delivery.

memes.label: "Religion in Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "Religion in Anna Karenina functions as a contested moral framework — deployed by characters as justification, consolation, and self-deception, while Tolstoy uses it to explore questions of faith, meaning, and moral responsibility."

memes.label: Rural Moral Superiority in Anna Karenina
memes.description: An analysis of Tolstoy's persistent contrast between rural and urban life in Anna Karenina — how the countryside functions as the novel's moral touchstone against which the corruption of the city is measured.

memes.label: Ryabinin
memes.description: A shrewd merchant who purchases Levin's forest at a bargain price, embodying the rising commercial class's encroachment on the traditional landed gentry's domain.

memes.label: Sappho Shtoltz
memes.description: A fashionable and witty socialite in Princess Betsy Tverskaya's Petersburg circle, named for the Greek poetess and known for her sharp, vivacious personality.

memes.label: "Sergius Ivanitch Koznishev"
memes.description: "Sergius Ivanitch Koznishev is Konstantin Levin's celebrated half-brother, a writer and intellectual whose abstract theorizing and failed late-life romance illuminate Tolstoy's critique of rationalism divorced from lived experience."

memes.label: Seryozha Karenin
memes.description: Seryozha (Sergei Alexeich) Karenin is the young son of Anna Karenina and Alexei Karenin, a bright and sensitive child whose existence binds Anna to her former life and serves as a focal point for her maternal love and guilt throughout the novel.

memes.label: Mihail Stepanovitch Snetkov
memes.description: Mihail Stepanovitch Snetkov is the incumbent marshal of the province, a nobleman of the old school whose defeat at the hands of a younger, more ambitious faction during the provincial elections dramatizes the changing political landscape of the Russian nobility.

memes.label: "Society and Morality in Anna Karenina"
memes.description: "Tolstoy's critique of aristocratic Russian society — its hypocrisies, its double standards, and the moral emptiness beneath its codes of propriety."

memes.label: Soden
memes.description: A German spa town where several characters in Anna Karenina seek treatment and socialize, serving as a backdrop for off-stage events and past connections.

memes.label: "St. Petersburg"
memes.description: "The imperial capital, home to Anna Karenina, her husband Karenin, and the world of high government and rigid social convention."

memes.label: "Stepan Arkadyevitch Oblonsky (Stiva)"
memes.description: "Stepan Arkadyevitch Oblonsky (Stiva) is Anna Karenina's brother, a charming but irresponsible aristocrat whose marital infidelity opens the novel, and whose social grace and financial carelessness embody the declining Russian nobility."

memes.label: Stremov
memes.description: Stremov is a prominent Petersburg official and political rival of Alexei Karenin, whose professional ascent and personal conduct in high society represent the worldly power dynamics against which Karenin's rigid moralism proves ineffectual.

memes.label: Nikolay Ivanovitch Sviazhsky
memes.description: District marshal and Levin's friend, a progressive landowner who hosts discussions on agriculture and rural reform.

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "The Cavalry Regiment"
memes.description: "Count Vronsky's regiment — the social-military world that shapes his identity, his code of honor, and his inability to fully abandon his world for Anna."

memes.label: "The Russian Orthodox Church"
memes.description: "The Russian Orthodox Church in Anna Karenina functions as both a social institution intertwined with the nobility and a genuine spiritual resource — its rituals, doctrines, and representatives shaping the moral landscape the novel's characters inhabit."

memes.label: "The Moscow Ball"
memes.description: "The fateful ball where Anna and Vronsky dance together, sparking the affair that drives the novel's central tragedy — and where Kitty's hopes are dashed."

memes.label: The Mowing
memes.description: The iconic scene in Anna Karenina where Konstantin Levin joins his peasants in mowing the fields, experiencing a rare moment of physical harmony, collective labor, and self-forgetfulness.

memes.label: "The Peasantry"
memes.description: "The peasantry in Anna Karenina represents Tolstoy's ideal of authentic, labor-based existence — the moral counterweight to aristocratic decadence and intellectual abstraction, embodied in Levin's agricultural work and his spiritual awakening among the muzhiks."

memes.label: "The Railway Worker's Death"
memes.description: "The ominous death of a railway worker at the Moscow station as Anna arrives — a moment of foreshadowing that bookends the novel's opening and closing tragedies."

memes.label: "The Railway Station"
memes.description: "The railway station is the novel's most symbolically charged setting — the site of Anna's first meeting with Vronsky, the railway worker's death that foreshadows her own, and the threshold between Moscow, Petersburg, and the rural world."

memes.label: The Railway as Fate Engine
memes.description: A systematic analysis of the railway motif in Anna Karenina — how Tolstoy uses trains, stations, and railway imagery as a structural device linking the novel's beginning, middle, and end into a unified meditation on fate, modernity, and death.

memes.label: "The Russian Nobility"
memes.description: "The Russian nobility in Anna Karenina is the hereditary aristocratic class whose social codes, economic decline, and moral contradictions form the novel's primary milieu of social critique."

memes.label: The Shcherbatsky Family
memes.description: The Shcherbatsky family as a narrative and thematic unit in Anna Karenina — their role as representatives of old Moscow nobility, the family's internal dynamics, and their function as a foil to the Oblonsky and Karenin households.

memes.label: "The Steeplechase"
memes.description: "The steeplechase at the Krasnoe Selo officers' race is a pivotal scene in Anna Karenina where Vronsky's horse Frou-Frou falls, foreshadowing the tragic trajectory of his affair with Anna."

memes.label: "Translated by Constance Garnett"
memes.description: "Anna Karenina, translated by Constance Garnett"

memes.label: Turovtsin
memes.description: A good-natured, simple-minded friend of Stepan Arkadyevitch Oblonsky, known for his hearty laugh and warm heart.

memes.label: Tushkevitch
memes.description: A charming and fashionable young man in Princess Betsy Tverskaya's social circle, known for his polished manners and ubiquitous presence at Petersburg social gatherings.

memes.label: "Varenka"
memes.description: "Mademoiselle Varenka, the adopted daughter and companion of Madame Stahl at the German spa, who becomes Kitty Shcherbatskaya's model of virtuous selflessness during her moral transformation in Part Two."

memes.label: "Vassenka Veslovsky"
memes.description: "A distant cousin of the Shcherbatsky family, a fashionable and cheerful young man from Petersburg whose uninvited visit to Pokrovskoe during Levin and Kitty's honeymoon creates comic tension, particularly through his flirtation with Kitty."

memes.label: Vassily Lukitch
memes.description: Seryozha Karenin's tutor, a kind-hearted and well-meaning man caught between the boy's longing for his mother and the formal discipline of the Karenin household.

memes.label: "Vozdvizhenskoe"
memes.description: "Vozdvizhenskoe is Alexei Vronsky's country estate, contrasting with Levin's Pokrovskoe — a model estate run on modern principles but lacking the organic connection to the land that characterizes Levin's approach."

memes.label: Yegorushka Korsunsky
memes.description: The dashing master of ceremonies at the Moscow ball, renowned as the finest waltzer in Petersburg whose energetic direction of the dancing shapes the ball's social dynamics.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: Comprehensive index of all characters in Leo Tolstoy's Anna Karenina — from major protagonists to minor servants and officials — with reference counts and quality ratings.

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Catalogue of key events and episodes in Anna Karenina — from social gatherings and romantic encounters to moments of crisis, revelation, and tragedy.

memes.label: Organizations
memes.description: Index of institutions and social bodies that structure the world of Anna Karenina — government ministries, military regiments, agricultural societies, and the formal organizations through which characters exercise power and pursue status.

memes.label: Places
memes.description: Directory of significant locations in Anna Karenina — Moscow and St. Petersburg drawing-rooms, country estates, railway stations, and the physical spaces where the novel's pivotal scenes unfold.

memes.label: Themes and Concepts
memes.description: Index of thematic and conceptual entries exploring the moral, social, and philosophical dimensions of Anna Karenina — love, marriage, faith, jealousy, death, and the search for meaning.
